prompt:
Incident review keeps eating half a day because the evidence for one user session is smeared across three log shapes: the edge gateways write plain text lines, the app tier writes key=value lines, and the identity service exports CSV. I want one tool, loglens.ps1 in the repo root, that ingests a whole capture directory and takes us from raw lines to an escalation shortlist. Acceptance is staged the same way the pipeline is: pwsh -NoProfile -File test_loglens.ps1 runs the parse, correlate and report suites (test_parse.ps1 / test_correlate.ps1 / test_report.ps1 -- all four files plus everything under fixtures/ are read-only). Build it with objects end to end: parse once into normalized records, correlate those records into sessions, report off the sessions. How you factor the code is up to you; the entry point and contracts below are fixed.

CLI: pwsh -NoProfile -File loglens.ps1 <normalize|sessions|escalations> -LogDir <dir>. Anything else on the command position => stderr 'usage: loglens.ps1 normalize|sessions|escalations -LogDir <dir>' and exit 64. -LogDir that is not an existing directory => stderr 'loglens: log directory not found: <value as given>' and exit 66.

Ingest (shared by all three commands). A capture directory has app/*.log, auth/*.csv, gateway/*.log. Scan sources in that order (app, auth, gateway), files in ordinal filename order, lines top to bottom. Line shapes:
- gateway: <ts> <host> <session> <client> "<METHOD> <path>" <status> <ms>ms, e.g. 2026-06-30T08:00:05Z gw01 S-1001 10.4.0.5 "GET /login" 200 45ms. METHOD is capital letters, status three digits, duration digits followed by ms. The client column is not carried forward.
- app: space-separated key=value tokens in any key order (e.g. ts=... host=... level=info session=S-1001 user=mora event=login-ok). ts/host/level/session/event are required, user is optional, unrecognized keys are ignored.
- auth: CSV with header exactly timestamp,host,session,user,outcome; the header is never a record; outcome is ok or fail.
Blank lines are skipped silently. Any other line that does not fit its source's shape (gateway line that does not match, app line with a bare token or a missing required key, auth row without exactly 5 fields) is skipped with one stderr line 'loglens: malformed line <bare filename>:<lineno>' (1-based line numbers counting every physical line, header included) -- and the run still exits by its normal rules.

Every kept line becomes one normalized record: Timestamp (the source string, ISO UTC), Source (app|auth|gateway), Host, Session, User ('' when the line has none -- gateway always, app without user=), Level, Event. Level: app uses its level field; gateway maps status >= 500 to error, 4xx to warn, else info; auth maps fail to warn, ok to info. Event: app uses its event field; gateway uses '<METHOD> <path>'; auth uses auth-ok / auth-fail.

normalize prints the records as NDJSON, one compact JSON object per line with keys exactly in the order Timestamp,Source,Host,Session,User,Level,Event, sorted by Timestamp, then Source, then Session, then Event (all ordinal). Exit 0.

sessions groups records by Session and prints one compact NDJSON object per session, Session-ordinal order, keys exactly Session,User,Start,End,DurationSeconds,Hosts,Sources,Events,Errors,AuthFailures. User = the User of the earliest record (normalize order) that has one, else ''. Start/End = min/max Timestamp. DurationSeconds = whole seconds between them (integer). Hosts and Sources = distinct values, ordinal-sorted, comma-joined. Events = record count. Errors = records with Level error. AuthFailures = auth records with Event auth-fail. Exit 0.

escalations evaluates each session against three independent rules and prints '<severity> <session> <reason>' lines: high when AuthFailures >= 3, reason 'repeated auth failures (<n>)'; medium when the session touched >= 4 distinct hosts, reason 'session roamed <n> hosts'; low when Errors >= 2, reason 'elevated error volume (<n>)'. A session can fire any number of rules. Output is grouped by severity in the order high, medium, low, session-ordinal within a group. At least one line => exit 65; nothing to report => empty stdout, exit 0.

Hard rules: no Format-* in any output path, no Get-Date (timestamps come from the logs), every sort ordinal, LF line endings.
notes: 6P9 item 2: log-intelligence capstone -- 13 multi-source fixture log files (text gateway lines, logfmt app lines, CSV auth exports) through normalize -> correlate -> escalate, with staged test suites (parse/correlate/report) aggregated by a master harness. Objects end-to-end; output pinned as compact NDJSON via [ordered]+ConvertTo-Json -Compress (probe-verified key-order stability). PowerShell-native pins: property-bag records with pinned key order, ordinal sorting everywhere (session/host/source joins), DateTimeOffset duration math with invariant culture, three-command CLI over one shared object ingest. Differentiation: ps-logsift (6P2-4) parses ONE app-log format into objects with a JSON report -- no multi-source normalization, no cross-source correlation, no policy layer; py-usage-sessions/py-log-analyzer are the wave-1-3 python analogs but text/dict based; go-logfmt is a codec, not a pipeline. Escalation thresholds are set so the medium rule (>=4 hosts) does not fire on an ordinary gateway+app+auth session (3 hosts).
